heading: Set Up the Environment
body:
To set up the environment, complete the following steps:

Create a new project directory, then install the required dependencies:

```python
mkdir langgraph-mongodb-ai-agent
cd langgraph-mongodb-ai-agent
pip install --quiet --upgrade python-dotenv langgraph langgraph-checkpoint-mongodb langgraph-store-mongodb langchain langchain-mongodb langchain-voyageai langchain-openai pymongo
```

> **Note:** Your project will use the following structure:
>
> ```text
> langgraph-mongodb-ai-agent
> ├── .env
> ├── config.py
> ├── search-tools.py
> ├── memory-tools.py
> ├── agent.py
> ├── main.py
> ```

Create a `.env` file in your project and specify
the following variables. Replace the placeholder values with valid
API keys and your MongoDB cluster's connection string.

```python
VOYAGE_API_KEY = "<voyage-api-key>"
OPENAI_API_KEY = "<openai-api-key>"
MONGODB_URI = "<connection-string>"
```

> **Note:** Replace `<connection-string>` with the connection string for your
> Atlas cluster or local Atlas deployment.
>
> <Tab tabid="cloud">
>   Atlas Cluster
>
>   Your connection string should use the following format:
>
>   ```
>   mongodb+srv://<db_username>:<db_password>@<clusterName>.<hostname>.mongodb.net
>   ```
>
>   To learn more, see [Driver](connect-via-driver).
> </Tab>
>
> Local or Self-Managed
>
> Your connection string should use the
> following format:
>
> ```
> mongodb://localhost:<port-number>/?directConnection=true
> ```
>
> To learn more, see [Connection Strings](manual/reference/connection-string).
